revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Himure Hachiman-gū]][[ja:日牟禮八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Himure Hachiman-gū
日牟禮八幡宮 | image = 近江八幡市にて 日牟禮八幡宮の拝殿 Himure-hachiman-gū 2012.8.26 - panoramio.jpg | caption = ''Heiden'' of Himure Hachiman-gū | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|54.7|N|137|1|16.8|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Himure Hachiman-gū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, Hime-gami | founded_by = c. [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] | established =c.131AD | date_destroyed = | location = Ōmihachiman-shi, Shiga-ken | website = {{Official website|https://himure.jp}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Himure Hachiman-gū'''|日牟禮八幡宮|}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Ōmihachiman|en|Ōmihachiman|qid=Q385461|lt=Ōmihachiman|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The city is named after this shrine, and it is located within the Omihachiman City Traditional Buildings Preservation Area. The shrine has two main festivals, the {{nihongo|'''Sagichō Matsuri'''|左義長まつり|}} and the {{nihongo|'''Hachiman Matsuri'''|八幡まつり|}} which are both designated [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Folk Cultural Properties|National Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]].{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/200086|title=近江八幡の火祭り|trans-title= Ōmihachiman festivals |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} ==History== The foundation of Himure Hachiman-gū is uncertain. According to the shine’s legend, it was founded in 131 AD when the semi-legendary {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|qid=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}} ordered [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] to establish a shrine to “Oshima no Okami” at this location. However, “Ōshima no Ōkami” is identified with [[Ōkuninushi]] and thus this legend may be confused with that of the {{nihongo|Ōshima Jinja Okitsushima Jinja|大嶋神社奥津嶋神社||}} which is located nearby. In 275 AD, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, visited {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|qid=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}, and stayed at a temporary palace at the built at the location of the present shrine. During his visit, he witnessed a mysterious phenomenon in which two suns appeared in the sky at the same time. The phenomenon was considered to herald an auspicious event, and a shrine called “Himure-no-yashiro” was constructed on the site. Emperor Ōjin was later identified as the avatar of the ''[[kami]]'' [[Hachiman]] and therefore the shrine came to be called the "Himure Hachiman-gū".{{cite web |url=https://himure.jp |title=Himure Hachiman-gū Official website |language=English |publisher=Himure Hachiman-gū |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} In 911, {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|en|Emperor Ichijō|qid=Q449540|lt=Emperor Ichijō|1=Emperor Ichijō}}, constructed a shrine on the northern peak of Mount Hachiman located behind this shrine as a branch of the [[Usa Jingū|Usa-Hachiman-gū]]. This was called the "Upper shrine", or ''Kami-no-yashiro'' with the original shrine at the foot of the mountain referred to as the "Lower shrine", or ''Shimo-no-yashiro''. In 1585, {{ill|Toyotomi Hidetsugu|en|Toyotomi Hidetsugu|qid=Q967501|lt=Toyotomi Hidetsugu|1=Toyotomi Hidetsugu}} merged the two shrines together and constructed {{ill|Hachimanyama Castle|en|Hachimanyama Castle|qid=Q10892157|lt=Hachimanyama Castle|1=Hachimanyama Castle}} on the site of the ''Kami-no-yashiro''. Although the castle was soon abandoned after he was ordered to commit ''{{ill|Seppuku|en|Seppuku|qid=Q133462|lt=Seppuku|1=Seppuku}}'', the {{ill|Jōkamachi|en|Jōkamachi|qid=Q909458|lt=castle town|1=Jōkamachi}} which had developed around the castle and the lower shrine flourished as many merchants relocated to this area after the ruin of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|qid=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}}'s {{ill|Azuchi Castle|en|Azuchi Castle|qid=Q786888|lt=Azuchi Castle|1=Azuchi Castle}}. These traveling merchants, known as ''Ōmi shōnin'', traded widely throughout Japan, and even overseas, and adopted this shrine as their guardian deity. The Himure Hachiman-gū has a {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|qid=Q687288|lt=votive tablet|1=Ema (Shinto)}} depicting a trading vessel between Japan and {{ill|Vietnam|en|Vietnam|qid=Q881|lt=Annam|1=Vietnam}} with prayers for a safe voyage which is a designated National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/1272 |title=安南渡海船額|trans-title= Annam Trading Vessel ema |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} In 1876, following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]] and the establishment of the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] under {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|qid=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}}, the shrine was officially designated a “village shrine”. It was promoted to the Rank of “prefectural shrine” in 1916. Then shrine is a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Ōmi-Hachiman Station|en|Ōmi-Hachiman Station|qid=Q1042782|lt=Ōmi-Hachiman Station|1=Ōmi-Hachiman Station}} on then {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|qid=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line|1=Biwako Line}}. ==Gallery== 近江八幡市にて 日牟禮八幡宮の鳥居 2012.8.26 - panoramio.jpg|Heiden and precincts Himure hachimangu01s3200.jpg|Romon Himure hachimangu06s3200.jpg|Noh Stage Himure hachimangu05s3200.jpg|Honden ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Himure-hachimangu}} *[https://himure.jp Official website]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/201/ Shiga - Biwako Visitor's Guide]{{in lang|ja}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[en:Himure Hachiman-gū]] {{Commons category|Himure-hachimangu}} [[ja:日牟禮八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11509681}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ōmihachiman, Shiga]]
